title: Lions Gate Entertainment (LGF.A) Acquires Global Platform eOne
text: Lions Gate Entertainment (LGF.A) announces that it has successfully completed the acquisition of eOne, a global content platform.

title: Hasbro Completes Sale of Entertainment One Film and Television Business to Lionsgate
text: PAWTUCKET, R.I.--(BUSINESS WIRE)---- $HAS #HAS--Hasbro Completes Sale of Entertainment One Film and Television Business to Lionsgate.

title: Lionsgate Closes Acquisition Of eOne From Hasbro
text: Lionsgate said today its acquisition of global entertainment platform eOne from Hasbro is done. The transaction announced last August adds 6,500 film and television titles to Lionsgate's large library; diversifies and strengthens its scripted and unscripted television businesses; extends its portfolio of brands and franchises; and expands its presence in Canada and the UK.

title: CORRECTING and REPLACING Hasbro Announces Quarterly Cash Dividend on Common Shares

title: Hasbro Announces Quarterly Cash Dividend on Common Shares
text: PAWTUCKET, R.I.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Hasbro Announces Quarterly Cash Dividend on Common Shares.

text: Here are some of the major companies whose stocks moved on the week's news.

title: The 7 Most Undervalued Growth Stocks to Buy in December
text: Stocks of a lot of well-known and historically great companies are on sale right now. Many marquee names haven't shared in this year's market rally and are in the red for 2023.

title: Hasbro (HAS) Shares Dip on Layoff Announcement & Industry Woes
text: Hasbro (HAS) announces lay-offs in sync with its cost-saving strategy. The company is cautious of challenging market conditions potentially extending into 2024.

title: Hasbro says headwinds will “persist into 2024” as it announces layoff
text: Hasbro Inc (NASDAQ: HAS) opened in the red today after revealing plans of trimming another 900 jobs globally. Hasbro expects weakness in 2024 The announcement arrives more than a month after the multinational warned of a weak holiday quarter this year as consumers remain cautious on discretionary spending.

title: Hasbro Layoffs 2023: What to Know About the Latest HAS Job Cuts
text: Toymaker Hasbro (NYSE: HAS ) cut its workforce by 20%, slashing over 1,100 jobs. It is eliminating offices in Providence, Rhode Island, after seeing sluggish sales persist into the holidays.

title: Hasbro cutting 1,100 jobs as toy sales slow
text: Hasbro announced it is laying off over 1,000 employees in order to cut costs as it continues to struggle with declining toy sales for consecutive quarters.

title: Hasbro axes 1,100 jobs as sales struggle in Christmas build-up
text: Hasbro (NASDAQ:HAS) Inc, the firm behind Monopoly, Play-Doh and Transformers action figures is laying off 1,100 employees as poor sales dragged into the holiday trading season.  The toymaker will axe its workforce by almost a fifth, having already cut 800 jobs earlier this year, just two weeks after before Christmas, in a decision it described as “a last resort”.

title: Hasbro to Cut 900 Positions as Toy Industry Headwinds Continue
text: Toy company Hasbro will be laying off another 900 people, about a year after saying it would eliminate 1,000 positions from its global workforce. The latest job cuts will be made over the next 18 to 24 months, Hasbro said in a Monday (Dec. 11) filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC).

title: Hasbro slashing 900 jobs as high inflation takes bite out of toy sales
text: "The headwinds we saw through the first nine months of the year have continued into holiday and are likely to persist into 2024," Hasbro's CEO said.

title: Hasbro Laying Off Over 1,000 As CEO Calls Market Headwinds “Stronger And More Persistent Than Planned”
text: Giant toymaker Hasbro is laying off 1,100 employees, following 800 staff cuts announced earlier this year, as CEO Chris Cocks cited a tough business climate that's been slow to recover. That's about 20% of the company's global workforce. “The market headwinds we anticipated have proven to be stronger and more persistent than planned.

title: Hasbro to cut 1,100 jobs despite Dungeons & Dragons parent thriving
text: Hasbro is laying off 1,100 employees, according to an SEC filing. The company behind franchises like Dungeons & Dragons and Transformers, Hasbro already laid off 800 employees in January.

title: 2023 Layoff Tracker: Hasbro Cuts 1,100 Employees
text: Toy manufacturer Hasbro will cut 1,100 employees, according to an internal memo released Monday, making it the latest major U.S. company to reduce its head count this year (see Forbes' layoff tracker from earlier this year here).

title: Hasbro laying off 1,100 workers as weak toy sales persist into holiday season
text: Hasbro, the toy brand behind My Little Pony and Transformers, had already laid off hundreds of employees earlier this year.

title: Toymaker Hasbro lays off 1,100 staff as holiday season fails to lift sales
text: Manufacturer of Monopoly, Play-Doh and Transformer cuts workforce by almost one-fifth as ‘last resort'

title: Hasbro, Mattel stocks fall after report of Hasbro layoffs amid toy sales slump
text: Hasbro Inc. HAS, +0.39% is cutting nearly 20% of its workforce, or about 1,100 employees, as a slump for toy and game sales continues into the holiday shopping period, the Wall Street Journal reported, citing a memo it viewed. The My Little Pony and Monopoly maker in October missed third-quarter earnings expectations and slashed its full-year outlook, citing a “softer toy outlook.

title: Is the Options Market Predicting a Spike in Hasbro (HAS) Stock?

title: Hasbro's Dividend Yield Pays As We Wait For Recovery
text: Hasbro is down 57% from its high in 2019, but we believe it to be oversold with strong recovery potential. The company is streamlining its operations and focusing on its core brands to unlock their value. Hasbro's Wizards of the Coast and Digital Gaming segment is experiencing strong growth, while the Consumer Products segment is facing challenges.

title: Starbreeze to develop and publish game based on the Dungeons & Dragons® IP
text: Starbreeze Entertainment today announces that it has licensed the world's greatest roleplaying game - Dungeons & Dragons® - to be used in the upcoming Project Baxter game. Starbreeze licenses the IP from Wizards of the Coast, the IP-holder of Dungeons & Dragons® and a division of Hasbro, Inc. The upcoming game is planned to be released in 2026, with Starbreeze acting both as developer and publisher.

title: Doing Your Holiday Shopping? These Stocks Might Make Great Gifts
text: It's that time of year when people are doing their holiday shopping and making their gift list — and checking it twice. Whether you're a parent planning gifts for your children of any age, including adult children, or you're thinking about gift ideas for other family members, friends or anyone else, you may be thinking about giving money or gift cards.
